content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

December 13, 1996

Vivian Love
City Clerk
Jay Thompson
Assistant City Clerk
City of West Hollywood
City Hall
8300 Santa Monica Boulevard
West Hollywood, California 90069-4314

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-96-312

Dear Ms. Love and Mr. Thompson:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign provisions of
Proposition 208.¹ Please note that the advice contained in this letter is subject to review and
modification by the Commission.

QUESTION

How will Proposition 208 affect local jurisdictions holding elections in the Spring of
1997, including contributions received prior to the effective date of Proposition 208,
January 1, 1997, in connection with a local election to be held in the City of West Hollywood on
March 4, 1997?

CONCLUSION

The contribution limits of Proposition 208's Section 85301 will be in effect in local
jurisdictions holding elections after January 1, 1997. However, the ballot pamphlet designation
requirements of Section 85602 will not apply to elections held before April 1997.

¹ All statutory references are to the Government Code. Proposition 208 substantially amends the Political
Reform Act of 1974, contained in sections 81000 - 91015. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18000
- 18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-96-312
Page 2

FACTS

The City of West Hollywood will have a local election on March 4, 1997. The city does
not have a campaign finance ordinance. The deadline for filing a declaration of candidacy for
such election was December 4, 1996. The deadline with an extension period (where no
incumbent files) was December 11, 1996. You are scheduled to print the sample ballot on
December 26, 1996. The statutory deadline for mailing the sample ballot to the voters for the
March 4, 1997, election is February 11, 1997.

ANALYSIS

You have inquired about the effect of Proposition 208 on your upcoming elections slated
for March 4, 1997. Specifically, you are concerned about contributions received by local
candidates prior to January 1, 1997, the effective date of Proposition 208. In your jurisdiction,
West Hollywood, there is no local ordinance establishing local contribution limits.

As you are aware, Proposition 208 presents many complex questions. Many of the
immediate transitional issues are addressed in the Johnson Advice Letter, No. A-96-316 (copy
enclosed), which discusses various issues concerning the use of contributions raised prior to
January 1, 1997.

This letter raises the issue of whether one or more provisions of Proposition 208,
including the contribution limits imposed by Section 85301, should be suspended in local
elections held on or before a certain date, in order to avoid confusion and partial, nonuniform
implementation of Proposition 208.

With regard to the contribution limits in Proposition 208, we believe several options
exist. First, the Commission can fully enforce the contribution limits, which become effective
January 1, 1997. Second, to ameliorate the harsh results and confusion that may result from
a completely new scheme at the end of the election cycle, the Commission may decide not to
enforce the contribution limits for elections that have opening dates for filing a declaration of
candidacy prior to January 1, 1997. The practical impact of this option would be to suspend the
application of the contribution limits for February, March, and April 1997 elections. Third,
under similar rationale, the Commission may decide not to enforce the contribution limits for
elections that have closing dates for filing a declaration of candidacy prior to January 1, 1997.
This would suspend the contribution limits for elections held in February and March of 1997.
Other options exist, of course. The Commission will discuss this issue at a special meeting
scheduled for December 20, 1996, and may adopt an emergency regulation as early as
December 30, 1996.

Our File No. A-96-312
Page 3

For purposes of your election, it is our view that the transitional rules discussed in the
Johnson Advice Letter affords candidates some flexibility to run their campaigns, without
running afoul of the plain language of Section 85301. The letter, among other things, advises
that contributions made prior to the effective date of Proposition 208 do not count toward the
contribution limits. Consistent with that letter, we conclude that as of January 1, 1997, the
contribution limits of Section 85301 apply to your March election, but contributions made for
that election in 1996 do not count against the applicable limit. Therefore, an individual who
contributes $1,000 in 1996 to a candidate on the ballot on March 4, 1997, may contribute up to
$100 or $250 in 1997 to the same candidate.

Another of Proposition 208's provisions that could significantly impact local filing
officers is Section 85602. We also address in this letter the applicability of that section to
elections being held this spring. Section 85602 requires local elections officers to prominently
designate on the ballot and in the ballot pamphlet and sample ballot those candidates who have
voluntarily agreed to expenditure ceilings. Pursuant to Section 85602, the Commission shall
prescribe by regulation the method or methods for such designations. The Commission has not
yet promulgated regulations concerning the designations. Since you are not in a jurisdiction that
has adopted voluntary expenditure limits, it does not appear that this section will affect you.
However, we note your printing deadlines, which may be similar for other jurisdictions. You are
scheduled to print the sample ballot on December 26, 1996, before Proposition 208 takes effect,
and the mailing to the voters must be no later than February 11, 1997. Given these facts,
we believe it would be difficult, if not impossible, for local filing officers to comply with this
requirement for elections held in February and March of 1997.

If you have questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Or, in the alternative, you may address the Commission regarding these conclusions and the
advice rendered in the Johnson Advice Letter at the December 20, 1996, special meeting.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Luisa Menchaca
Senior Staff Counsel, Legal Division
Enclosure
SGC:LM:ak

March 4, 1997?
